differences.title: 1. Rendering Location
differences.leftDesc: Renders on the server, sends HTML. Reduces client bundle size.
differences.rightDesc: Renders in the browser after hydration. Enables interactivity and state.

differences.title: 2. Bundle Size
differences.leftDesc: Code is not shipped to the client, reducing initial load.
differences.rightDesc: Code is downloaded and executed by the browser.

differences.title: 3. Data Fetching
differences.leftDesc: Can directly access server-side resources, databases, or file systems.
differences.rightDesc: Must fetch data via APIs or client-side fetching libraries.

differences.title: 4. Interactivity
differences.leftDesc: Cannot use browser-specific APIs or event handlers directly.
differences.rightDesc: Can use all browser APIs, state management, and event listeners.

differences.title: 5. State Management
differences.leftDesc: Primarily for UI rendering, not managing client-side interactive state.
differences.rightDesc: Manages UI state, user interactions, and dynamic updates.

differences.title: 6. Dependencies
differences.leftDesc: Can import modules not available in the browser environment.
differences.rightDesc: Imports must be compatible with browser execution.

differences.title: 7. Reusability
differences.leftDesc: Can be used by both server and client components, sharing logic.
differences.rightDesc: Typically used for highly interactive UI elements and user interfaces.
